cases.judge: The patch is well-scoped and keeps the required `quote_stay`/`reserve_stay` APIs, but its booking semantics are still procedural and fails a key invariant by mutating inventory per raw night entry rather than robust stay-night semantics.

The patch implements core scheduling and late-fee checks, but it remains orchestration-by-dicts with helper-driven procedural flow and misses deeper domain collaboration (notably no internal domain objects and no appointment-window provenance from scheduling).
The patch satisfies the required APIs and core ticketing behaviors, but it stays in a procedural helper-style style with weak domain object collaboration.
The patch adds a domain-style structure with required APIs but fails the payment-gating rule by mutating room inventory across the tested flow, so the reservation behavior is not yet correct.

The patch models core domain concepts and keeps the public API surface, but it violates the EO naming/proscription expectations with a `Service`-centered check-in orchestrator and still fails a core scheduling behavioral case.

The patch delivers the requested domain behavior in a compact way, with good object collaboration and DTO boundaries, but includes an unused object in the model and still relies on raw dictionaries for key collaborators, slightly weakening EO purity.

title: EO app skeleton holdout run 1, Spark fast
